### Post by tech_cheetah on 2008-06-27
Everytime I start my system, the resolution is 800x600 although the standard resolution for my desktop is 1280x800. Everytime I start the system I have to manually set the resolution to 1280x800.
I am using Nvidia 173.xx drivers and my xorg.xonf has following entries :
```

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder63)  Thu Jun  5 00:10:21 PDT 2008

    ModeLine       "1280x768@60" 80.14 1280 1344 1480 1680 768 769 772 795 -hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x720@60" 74.48 1280 1336 1472 1664 720 721 724 746 -hsync +vsync

### Post by Forlong on 2008-06-27
It's most probably because of this modeline:
```
ModeLine "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
```

---

### Post by Rhubarb on 2008-06-27
press Alt + F2, then enter this in:
```
gksu displayconfig-gtk
```

This will give you a nice window where you can set your resolution / video card drivers / screen type.
Once finished, press OK then log out or restart Ubuntu and you should then have the proper resolution / video drivers working.

---

### Post by jp734 on 2008-06-27
Are you sure your card can handle 1280 x 800 at 24 depth. Try changing 24 to 16 and see what happens.

### Post by tech_cheetah on 2008-06-28
It was working fine until I installed updates on Thursday.

---

### Post by jp734 on 2008-06-29
Everything looks fine with your xorg.conf file but I noticed that it was created by FailsafeDexconf. Happened more likely after you updated your system. People are having all sorts of issues lately after updating the system. I would recommend reinstalling (or updating) your video driver.
